summary: Backend-only testing for Apartment Maintenance app. Wrote /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covering health, auth (admin login, /auth/me, bad creds, no-token), admin CRUD for users & units (incl. role scope on /units), tickets full lifecycle (create with base64 images, auto-assign to least-loaded maintenance, list scoping per role, 403 cross-tenant, 403 cross-maintenance, tenant cannot change status, admin reassign + invalid assignee 400), comments auto-transitioning open->in_progress, status resolution, stats counts, and the 3/day tenant ticket limit returning 429. 23/23 tests pass against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.

minor.issue: Allows empty password whitespace only via Pydantic (only checks falsy). Low risk since admin-controlled.
Returns [] for maintenance users. PRD says maintenance only needs assigned tickets, so acceptable but worth confirming if maintenance UI needs unit info (currently embedded in enriched tickets).
CORS is allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False; works for token-in-header model but does not match the playbook's httpOnly-cookie pattern. Not a bug for current Bearer auth design.

action_items: (Optional) Consider strengthening password validation in /api/users (min length, non-whitespace).
(Optional) Add brute-force lockout if you intend to follow auth playbook strictly (current design uses stateless JWT + Bearer header so playbook cookie/lockout items partly N/A).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses sync hash_password/verify_password (bcrypt) inside async endpoints. Under heavy load this blocks the event loop. Consider running in a threadpool (run_in_threadpool) — non-blocking now, but worth flagging.
Daily ticket limit uses today_start in UTC; tenants in non-UTC timezones may perceive the 3/day window differently. Document or make TZ configurable.
JWT_SECRET is committed in .env (dev only). Make sure production rotates it; otherwise tokens are forge-able if leaked.
Admin list /api/users returns the full user document including national_id, contract_number, phone — fine for admin role, but the cleanup helper depends only on _id/password_hash. OK.
server.py is a single 506-line file; still manageable but consider splitting routes (auth/users/units/tickets) once it grows.
test_credentials: admin/admin123 (auto-seeded). Tenants/maintenance created at test runtime with TEST_ prefixed usernames and cleaned up in fixture teardown.
seed_data_creation: Per-session fixture creates 2 units (TEST_<suffix>_101/102), 2 tenants, 1 maintenance. Auto-cleanup deletes tickets/users/units on teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Each run uses a fresh uuid suffix so reruns don't collide. If you add new endpoints, extend this file rather than creating new ones. Frontend not tested (per request).
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All requested behaviors verified including: 3/day rate limit (429), auto-assign to least-loaded maintenance, RBAC 403 cross-tenant & cross-maintenance, comment auto-transition to in_progress, admin cannot delete admin role, stats counts, and base64 image storage.
